channel.title: Nutrition Science on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Nutrition Science on MinWeb

item.title: Why Nutrition Misinformation Is Everywhere (And How to Cope)
item.description:
It’s 7:00 AM. You’re scrolling through your feed, clutching your coffee, and in the span of thirty seconds, you’re told your breakfast is “toxic,” an $80 supplement is the only thing standing between you and optimal health, and a “secret” ingredient in your yogurt is causing systemic inflammation.

You feel a twinge of anxiety. You want to be healthy, but you’re confused. And honestly? I don’t blame you. Eight out of ten Americans struggle to distinguish fact from fiction when it comes to nutrition. It’s not that you aren’t smart—it’s that you are swimming in an ocean of manufactured confusion.

item.title: Why We’re Still Hooked on Full-Sugar Soda
item.description:
If you’ve ever wandered down the soda aisle, you’ve likely felt a twinge of bewilderment. We live in an era where the health risks of sugar are common knowledge. We know that sugar-sweetened beverages (SSBs) trigger rapid insulin spikes, contribute to Type 2 diabetes, damage the liver, and hijack the brain’s dopamine system to create genuine dependency.

And yet, the global market for carbonated soft drinks is projected to reach $473 billion by 2025. Even more shocking? As of 2024, full-sugar sodas still hold over half of that market.
